rows.snippet: New Features for Laserfiche Directory Server 9.2. 5 years ago 9.2 Summary This article highlights new features for Directory Server 9.2. Directory Server Web Administration Console The Directory Server Web Administration Console presents a W... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013536/new-features-for-laserfiche-di ...
rows.title: New Features for Laserfiche Directory Server 9.2.
rows.detailTitle: New Features for Laserfiche Directory Server 9.2.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in New Features for Laserfiche Directory Server 9.2. Product: Directory Server 9.2 KB: 1013536 Published: October 6, 2014 Last Updated: August 14, 2020 Summary This article highlights new features for Directory Server 9.2. Directory Server Web Administration Console The Directory Server Web Administration Console presents a Web application for managing licenses in a Directory Server site. Use a Web browser to view an updated graphical user interface for retrieving a primary license from Laserfiche, register and assign named user licenses to Windows Active Directory user accounts or Novell eDirectory user accounts, register application instances, downloading license files, and more. Organizations Laserfiche Directory Server offers additional options for sorting available licenses into different containers for easier management. Organize registered directory accounts into different Laserfiche Directory Server organizations. Use these organizations to reserve blocks of licenses for specific departments. The administration website contains a page for subdividing the total number of licenses defined in a primary license among different organizations. When assigning named user licenses to registered directory accounts that are members of an organization, the maximum number of available named user licenses will be limited to the number reserved for that specific organization. Similar limits can be applied to application licenses for an organization. Security Directory Server 9.2 introduces more granular security controls for limiting access to portions of a licensing site. At the site-level, assign rights to control who can access and view a licensing site and download application licenses as well as control who can manage the identity providers (e.g., Active Directory or Novel eDirectory server) registered with a Directory Server licensing site. At the organization level, grant specific accounts the ability to manage licenses for a specific organization. Changes from License Manager 8 Directory Server stores licensing information within a Microsoft SQL Server database. When creating a Directory Server licensing site, you will be prompted to specify a valid Microsoft SQL Server instance. Microsoft SQL Server 2008 R2 Express is included in the installation package. Related Links 1013535 Release Notes for Laserfiche Directory Server 9.2 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: List of Changes for Directory Server 9.2.1. 5 years ago https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013625/list-of-changes-for-directory-server-9-2-1- Directory Server 9.2.1 Summary This article highlights the changes for Laserfiche Directory Serve... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013625/list-of-changes-for-directory- ...
rows.title: List of Changes for Directory Server 9.2.1.
rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for Directory Server 9.2.1.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith List of Changes for Directory Server 9.2.1. Product: Directory Server 9.2.1 KB: 1013625 Published: May 21, 2015 Last Updated: July 27, 2020 Summary This article highlights the changes for Laserfiche Directory Server 9.2.1. New features include: Options to export CSV files with user listing and current license allocation information. (122807) When viewing license information for an organization, you can now view a quick summary of license allocation. The option to enable or disable SSL for the default identity provider that is automatically created for each Directory Server site. Additional updates include: When viewing registered instances of an application, you no longer need to expand the details of a registered instance to see the option to get a license file. Directory Server now sends an immediate notification to Laserfiche Servers when an Active Directory synchronization rule grants or removes a license automatically. (126692) Sometimes, members of the Laserfiche Directory Service Administrators local group would receive an "Access Denied" error message when trying to view the administration site. This issue is now resolved. (125885) The "Application version" drop-down list now properly displays a value when registering certain version 9.0 products. (125795) The licensing site creation process now allows you to select a pre-existing blank SQL database. (124882) the Register Application Instance page now displays the number of remaining Forms Portal licenses. (124148) Page loading speed is improved when there are a large number of registered users. (124035) The site now functions properly when adding large numbers of user accounts manually. (122822) Directory Server can now display more verbose error messages when it encounters issues renewing a primary license. (121493) In certain situations, the administration site may display an HTTP 404 error when installed on 64-bit version of Windows Server 2008. This issue is now resolved. (118657) More Information Directory Server 9.2.1 includes hotfixes released previously for Directory Server 9.2. 1013599 List of Changes for Directory Server 9.2 Hotfix 1013599. 1013576 List of Changes for Directory Server 9.2 Hotfix 1013576. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: A "HTTP 500.19" Error Occurs When Loading Laserfiche 8 Web Applications Hosted on a 64-bit Server with Windows Server Update Services. 15 years ago A "HTTP 500.19" Error Occurs When Loading Laserfiche 8 Web Applications Hosted on a 64-bit Server with Windows Server Update Services. October 4, 2010 | KB: 1012684 https://support ..... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1012684/a-http-500-19-error-occurs ...
rows.title: A "HTTP 500.19" Error Occurs When Loading Laserfiche 8 Web Applications Hosted on a 64-bit Server with Windows Server Update Services.
rows.detailTitle: A "HTTP 500.19" Error Occurs When Loading Laserfiche 8 Web Applications Hosted on a 64-bit Server with Windows Server Update Services.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in A "HTTP 500.19" Error Occurs When Loading Laserfiche 8 Web Applications Hosted on a 64-bit Server with Windows Server Update Services. Product: Web Access 8, WebLink 8, Audit Trail 8 KB: 1012684 Published: October 4, 2010 Last Updated: October 4, 2010 Summary A "HTTP 500.19" Error Occurs When Loading Laserfiche 8 Web Applications Hosted on a 64-bit Server with Windows Server Update Services. HTTP Error 500.19 - Internal Server Error The requested page cannot be accessed because the related configuration data for the page is invalid. InformationModule: DynamicCompressionModule Error Code: 0x8007007e Cause This issue can occur when loading 32-bit ASP.NET Web applications after installing the 64-bit version of Windows Server Update Services (WSUS). WSUS enables the XPress compression scheme in IIS, but the 64-bit installation of WSUS only installs the 64-bit version of the compression scheme module. Workaround See the following MSDN blog spot for troubleshooting information on HTTP 500.19 Errors in IIS 7. Troubleshooting HTTP 500.19 Errors in IIS 7 See Scenario 6 for details on workarounds to either disable the XPress compression scheme, configure IIS to only load the 64-bit version of the module for 64-bit application pools, or to use a 32-bit version of the compression module. 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: An "Error executing SQL command [7050]." Error Occurs When Copying or Moving a Document. 18 years ago An "Error executing SQL command [7050]." Error Occurs When Copying or Moving a Document. August 20, 2007 | KB: 1011870 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1011870/an-quot-... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1011870/an-error-executing-sql< ...
rows.title: An "Error executing SQL command [7050]." Error Occurs When Copying or Moving a Document.
rows.detailTitle: An "Error executing SQL command [7050]." Error Occurs When Copying or Moving a Document.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith An "Error executing SQL command [7050]." Error Occurs When Copying or Moving a Document. Product: Laserfiche Server - Team (MSDE) 7, Laserfiche Server - United (MS SQL) 7 KB: 1011870 Published: August 20, 2007 Last Updated: August 20, 2007 Summary When you attempt to copy or move a document with a template that contains fields such that the total length of all the field names exceeds 2000 characters, you may receive the following error message: Error executing SQL command [7050]. In your repository's error.log file, you may see entries similar to the following: [7050] Copy document failed (tocid=x, volid=y, destid=z): Unclosed quotation mark before the character string ''partial field name''. Resolution There is a hotfix available for Laserfiche Server 7.2.1. The fix is a SQL script that updates a stored procedure to increase a variable limit to 4000 characters. To update your Laserfiche Server Click the following link to download a zip file containing the hotfix file. Hotfix_SCR32531.zip Extract 32531.sql to a temporary location. Run the 32531.sql script on your SQL database. Click Start and then click Run. In the Run dialog box, type the following and then click OK to run the script. osql -E -S SQLServerInstance -d "DatabaseName" -i "PathTo32531.sql" SQLInstance is the name of your SQL Server instance name. DatabaseName is the name of the main SQL database associated with your Laserfiche repository. PathTo32531.sql is the full path (including the file name and extension) to the extracted 32531.sql file. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: A "Failed to load thumbnails. Error executing SQL command [7050]" Error Occurs When Opening a Document. 19 years ago A "Failed to load thumbnails. Error executing SQL command [7050]" Error Occurs When Opening a Document. August 25, 2006 | KB: 1011015 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1011015/a-failed-to-load-thumbnails-er ...
rows.title: A "Failed to load thumbnails. Error executing SQL command [7050]" Error Occurs When Opening a Document.
rows.detailTitle: A "Failed to load thumbnails. Error executing SQL command [7050]" Error Occurs When Opening a Document.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith A "Failed to load thumbnails. Error executing SQL command [7050]" Error Occurs When Opening a Document. Product: Laserfiche 7 KB: 1011015 Published: September 14, 2005 Last Updated: August 25, 2006 Summary When opening a document, you may receive the following error message: Failed to load thumbnails. Error executing SQL command [7050] In most cases, the thumbnails will still load after this message has been received. Cause This issue occurs when an expansion database is not successfully attached, or when if it is attached under the wrong name. Expansion databases are created when using MSDE and Microsoft SQL Server: Personal Edition. Expansion databases store thumbnail information and word location data. If an expansion database is not attached, or is attached under a different name, Laserfiche will be unable to access thumbnail information. Resolution Expansion databases must be attached to the SQL server. For instructions on correctly attaching your expansion database, see the following article: 1011022 HOW TO: Attaching Expansion Databases to MSDE or SQL Server Personal Edition. More Information After the error message has been received, the thumbnails will usually appear. This is because the client first tries to retrieve the thumbnail data from the expansion database. If it is unable to do so, it will then attempt to regenerate the thumbnails. Related Links For more information about expansion database files, please see the following Laserfiche Knowledge Base article: 1000519: INFO: MSDE and SQL Server Personal Edition Database File Information. For other issues with the same root cause and resolution, please see the following Laserfiche Knowledge Base articles: 1011023 ERROR: A "Failed writing data to repository [7050]" Error Occurs When OCRing. 1011038 ERROR: An "Error determining word locations. Error executing SQL command [7050]" Error Occurs When Applying Linked Text Annotations. For information regarding other thumbnail display issues with different root causes, please see the following Laserfiche Knowledge Base articles: 1000744 ERROR: A "Failed to load thumbnails. Cannot generate thumbnail [7582]" Error Occurs When Opening a Document. 1000630 ISSUE: Thumbnails Do Not Display When Opening a Document. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.detailTitle: An "An Installation package for the product Microsoft SQL Server Native Client cannot be found." Error Occurs When Installing SQL Server 2005 Express.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in An "An Installation package for the product Microsoft SQL Server Native Client cannot be found." Error Occurs When Installing SQL Server 2005 Express. Product: Laserfiche 8, Laserfiche 9 KB: 1012658 Published: August 9, 2010 Last Updated: August 9, 2010 Summary When using the Microsoft SQL Server 2005 Express installation included with Laserfiche 8 to install a "Laserfiche" instance of SQL Server, you may receive the following error message: An installation package for the product Microsoft SQL Native Client cannot be found. Try the installation again using a valid copy of the installation package ''Sqlncli.msi''. Workaround There is a known issue with the SQL Server 2005 Express installation where an existing SQL Native Client installation may prevent the installation from running. As a workaround, uninstall the existing SQL Native Client by using the Windows Add or Remove Programs before running the SQL Server 2005 Express installation. Related Links See the following Microsoft Knowledge Base article for more information: 910229 SQL Server 2005 Express Edition Readme 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark
